ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FHM covers fish soup generically; no Malay-specific fish head soup code exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for Malay fish head soup. Left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of Sup Kepala Ikan is one bowl of approximately 500 g, comprising the fish head pieces, vegetables, and broth, typically served alongside steamed white rice.

nutrient_source.title: Nutritional Composition of Malaysian Foods — IMR Malaysia MyFCD; supplemented by USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (Fish, mixed species, cooked, moist heat) for micronutrient cross-reference
nutrient_source.organization: Institute for Medical Research (IMR) Malaysia — MyFCD; United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Sup Kepala Ikan exists in MyFCD. Values are adapted from IMR MyFCD entries for fish-based soups and cooked fish (red snapper / grouper), cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for cooked fish (NDB 15264, mixed species, moist heat). Macros and micronutrients are consistent with the input AI-generated values, which fall within the plausible range for a lightly spiced fish head broth at approximately 65 kcal/100 g. Saturated fat estimated at 0.6 g/100 g based on typical lean fish head composition.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous estimate based on fish-based clear soup with low-starch vegetables
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Sup Kepala Ikan. GI of 25 is an analogous estimate based on fish-based clear soups with low-starch vegetables (tomatoes, okra) and high protein content, consistent with the University of Sydney GI Database category for protein-dominant, low-carbohydrate savoury soups. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (25 × 12.5) / 100 = 3.1 (carbohydrate per 500 g serving = 2.5 × 5 = 12.5 g).

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from fish stock and added salt (250 mg/100 g; ~1250 mg per 500 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size or request reduced-salt preparation, as a standard 500 g serving provides approximately 1250 mg sodium — over half the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~25), low carbohydrate content (2.5 g/100 g), and high protein (9.5 g/100 g) supporting glycaemic control
type2_diabetes.guidance: Sup Kepala Ikan is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without added sugar; its low GI and high protein content support stable postprandial blood glucose, though sodium intake from the broth should be monitored.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Beneficial lean fish protein and potential omega-3 fatty acids from fish head, offset by elevated sodium content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The lean fish protein and anti-inflammatory spices (ginger, galangal) in Sup Kepala Ikan may support cardiovascular health, but the high sodium content (~1250 mg per serving) warrants caution for individuals with cardiovascular disease who require strict sodium restriction.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Collagen-rich fish head and anti-inflammatory aromatics (ginger, galangal, lemongrass)
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The collagen from fish head and anti-inflammatory compounds in ginger and galangal may provide modest benefit for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis; this dish can be included as part of an anti-inflammatory dietary pattern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Sup Kepala Ikan (Fish Head Soup) is a traditional Malay soup dish built around the head of a large fish — most commonly red snapper (ikan merah) or grouper (kerapu). The broth is prepared by simmering the fish head with aromatic spices including ginger, lemongrass, galangal, and tamarind or lime juice, producing a clear to lightly cloudy broth with a characteristic balance of spicy, sour, and deeply savoury flavours. Vegetables such as tomatoes, okra (lady's finger), and fresh chilies are added during cooking, contributing colour, texture, and micronutrients. The fish head yields tender, gelatinous meat from the cheeks, collar, and eye socket area, which is prized for its rich texture and flavour. Nutritionally, Sup Kepala Ikan is a high-protein, low-calorie dish at approximately 65 kcal per 100 g, with 9.5 g protein, 2.2 g fat, and only 2.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g. It provides meaningful amounts of selenium (18.5 mcg), vitamin B12 (1.8 mcg), niacin (2.5 mg), phosphorus (140 mg), and potassium (220 mg). Its low glycemic index (~25) makes it suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. The primary clinical caution is its sodium content (~250 mg/100 g), which can be significant at a standard 500 g serving. The dish is commonly served with steamed white rice for lunch or dinner and is a staple of Malay home cooking and hawker culture across Malaysia and Singapore.
common_user_queries: Is Sup Kepala Ikan good for diabetics?
Is fish head soup high in sodium?
What fish is used in Sup Kepala Ikan?
How is Sup Kepala Ikan prepared?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Sup Kepala Ikan exists in IMR MyFCD or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are adapted from the closest approved-source equivalents (IMR MyFCD fish-based soups; USDA SR Legacy cooked fish) and are consistent with the AI-generated input values, which fall within the plausible nutritional range for this dish. GI is an analogous estimate. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, cholesterol, and moisture data for this traditional Malay fish head soup preparation are not individually reported in IMR MyFCD or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as of 2026-04-16; these fields cannot be reliably derived from available approved sources for this specific dish preparation. 2. saturated_fat_g (0.6 g/100 g) is estimated from typical lean fish head composition based on USDA SR Legacy data for cooked grouper/red snapper. 3. GI of 25 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database category for protein-dominant, low-carbohydrate savoury fish soups; no direct measurement exists for this dish. 4. All core macros (protein_g: 9.5, fat_g: 2.2, carbohydrate_g: 2.5) are non-zero and nutritionally plausible for a fish-based broth soup; no zero-macro justification required. 5. Sodium content is notably high at 250 mg/100 g (approximately 1250 mg per 500 g serving); clinical caution is warranted for hypertension and cardiovascular disease patients. 6. The dish is classified as halal as it contains only fish and plant-based aromatics with no pork or alcohol ingredients in the standard preparation. 7. Aliases self-check: all strings in aliases_by_language verified to appear verbatim in the flat aliases array — Fish Head Soup, Malay Fish Head Soup, Spiced Fish Head Broth, Fish Head Clear Soup, 鱼头汤, 魚頭湯, Sup Kepala Ikan, Ikan Kepala Sup, மீன் தலை சூப், மீன் தலை குழம்பு, मछली के सिर का सूप all confirmed present in flat aliases.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: energy_kcal (65.0), protein_g (9.5), fat_g (2.2), carbohydrate_g (2.5) all present and numeric. nutrient_source cites IMR MyFCD and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as approved sources. Values are within plausible range for a fish-based broth soup.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (0.5), sodium_mg (250.0), saturated_fat_g (0.6), sugar_g (0.8) all present as numeric values and plausible for this dish.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index (25), gi_category ('low'), glycemic_load_per_serving (3.1), and gi_source all present. GI sourced from University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2021 as analogous estimate with documented basis. GI is not null or zero.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn closest_parent match FOODON_03315595 ('fish soup') and FoodEx2 closest_parent match A0FHM ('Fish soup') provided with honest gap notes explaining no exact Malay fish head soup code exists. SNOMED CT left null with gap note. No codes invented.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description is well over 200 characters (~1,450 characters), covers preparation method, taste/texture, cultural context, clinical relevance, and key nutrients. common_user_queries and ingredient_synonyms are comprehensive.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions present: Hypertension (high concern — sodium), Type 2 Diabetes (low concern — low GI, high protein), Cardiovascular Disease (moderate concern — sodium offset by lean fish protein). Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence. human_condition_name 'Type 2 Diabetes' used correctly.
